purpose: Describes the bifurcated monetary-fiscal regime where the front-end of the yield curve follows conventional monetary policy while the long-end is dominated by fiscal supply dynamics, with distinct implications for each segment.
components: Front-end (2Y): conventional monetary policy transmission, negative stock-bond correlation, Fed credibility intact
Long-end (30Y): fiscal supply dominance, positive stock-bond correlation, term premium driven by supply/demand composition
Transition zone (5-10Y): mixed regime, 5Y5Y forward as regime indicator, fiscal wedge observable in cross-country comparison
Forward guidance horizon: credible within 6 months, structurally non-credible beyond
Butterfly spread (5s10s30s): potential sovereign stress signal within the split regime
current_assessment: Split regime is active. 2Y-SPX correlation -0.15 to -0.30, 30Y-SPX +0.05 to +0.15. 5Y5Y forward at 3.8-4.2% includes estimated fiscal wedge vs German 5Y5Y at 2.5-2.8%. MOVE index elevated at 95-130 confirms degraded transmission across the curve.
limitations: Only two historical resolution precedents (1965-69, 2003-06), N=2
Precise maturity at which regime transitions is unstable and time-varying
Current fiscal configuration (6.5% deficit at sub-4% unemployment) has zero peacetime precedent
Resolution path unknown — front-end could follow long-end into fiscal dominance, or long-end could normalize
r* vs term premium separation may be impossible with available data, complicating measurement
